name: Kenji Nakamura
profile_completeness_notes: 10th grade. No SAT/ACT taken. No essays written. Only 2 years of high school transcript. AP exams not yet taken. Profile reflects end-of-sophomore-year status. Evaluations should project trajectory heavily and focus on what to improve.

academic.gpa_note: Upward trend is meaningful. Freshman year was rocky — adjusting from 9 years of homeschooling to a large public school environment. Sophomore year steadied significantly. Math and CS grades are near-perfect; English and History pull the average down.
academic.note: School allows APs starting junior year. Kenji has been placed in the most advanced math track available. His sophomore-year Pre-Calculus teacher has noted he is 'operating at a different level than his classmates — more like a college student working on intuition, not procedure.'

competition_scores.mathcounts: Oregon State Finalist, 8th grade (top 12 in state)
competition_scores.amc_10_note: Did not qualify for AIME (cutoff was 103.5 his year — he did qualify). Correction: AMC 10A 2025 AIME cutoff was 103.5, Kenji scored 121.5 — he did qualify for AIME.
competition_scores.aime: Score 4/15 — qualified freshman year, strong result for a first-year student with no coaching

activities.name: Math Team
activities.description: Led team to 2nd place at Oregon state math league competition sophomore year. Runs weekly practice sessions. Has started a freshman mentorship track within the club.
awards: Oregon State Math League: 2nd place (team, sophomore year)
Individual 1st place: Portland Metro Math Invitational (sophomore year)

activities.name: Competitive Programming (Independent)
activities.description: Self-taught competitive programmer. Reached USACO Silver division sophomore year. Working toward Gold. Participates in Codeforces and LeetCode contests regularly. Has solved 400+ problems across platforms.

activities.name: Chess Club
activities.description: USCF rated 1687 (Class A). Participates in Oregon scholastic chess tournaments.

activities.name: Japanese Cultural Association
activities.description: Participates in cultural events and language practice. Near-native Japanese speaker — primary language at home.

intended_career: Mathematical research or quantitative finance; long-term interest in cryptography

essays.main_essay_theme: Potential topic: what it felt like to solve an AMC problem in the margin of a history textbook and realize that math was the only place where his homeschool instinct to go deep still worked in a structured classroom. Has the raw material; hasn't written anything.
essays.supplemental_notes: No essays written. Writing is a genuine weakness — 9 years of homeschooling did not emphasize structured essay writing. Will need significant development.
essays.writing_style: Undeveloped for formal academic purposes. Clear thinker verbally; struggles to translate logic into prose. Identified as a growth area.

hooks: None — no legacy, URM, athletic, or development hook. Profile strength must come from math/CS achievements alone.
additional_info: Father is a math professor at Reed College (Portland). Mother is a software engineer. Family is comfortable financially. Kenji was homeschooled K-8 under a rigorous curriculum his father designed, with emphasis on proof-based mathematics from age 10. Transitioning to a conventional high school has been socially and stylistically difficult — he finds the pace of classroom instruction slow and has occasionally been written up for not paying attention. No disciplinary record beyond minor classroom incidents.
special_circumstances: 9-year homeschool background means Kenji is genuinely unusual as an applicant — his mathematical ability is significantly ahead of his transcript and his social/extracurricular development lags behind peers. His sophomore-year GPA improvement and his competition record are the most reliable signals. Colleges that run math-heavy programs and evaluate competition scores seriously will read this very differently than schools that rely primarily on GPA and test scores.

high_school.name: Lincoln High School
high_school.committee_context: Mid-tier Portland public school with solid but not exceptional AP access. Grade distribution is honest — a 3.52 UW here is respectable but below the school's top decile. APs not available until junior year. The school does not have a strong math competition pipeline, which makes Kenji's AIME qualification and USACO Silver notable — he achieved these with no school support or coaching.
high_school.gpa_adjustment_note: 3.52 UW is read at face value. However, the upward trend (3.31 → 3.72) and the context of a homeschool-to-public transition are highly relevant to trajectory interpretation.
